cross_unit_queries.query: experiments with chamber pressure between 200 and 400 kPa
low pressure measurements between 5 and 50 kPa
tests at near-atmospheric pressure around 100 kPa
vacuum or low-pressure systems below 10 kPa
pressure in the range of 50000 to 100000 Pa
moderate pressure between 500 kPa and 2 MPa
pipe or nozzle diameter between 20 and 60 mm
mesh or grid spacing below 1 mm
small specimen width between 5 and 15 mm
displacements or deflections between 0.05 and 5 mm
reagent or sample mass between 50 and 500 mg
electrode or thin film mass below 50 mg
batch or component mass between 0.1 and 1 g
sub-gram reagent quantities between 100 and 1000 mg
reaction or test duration between 1 and 30 minutes
long simulations or experiments above 10 hours
simulation or observation around 30 to 60 min
high wind or flow speed between 30 and 80 m/s
vehicle or wind speed around 100 to 250 km/h

same_unit_queries.query: modulus in the 50 to 150 GPa range
distances in the 1 to 50 km range

formula_queries.query: Newton's second law force equals mass times acceleration
Einstein mass-energy equivalence E equals mc squared
kinetic energy one half mv squared
ideal gas law PV equals nRT
Reynolds number ratio of inertial to viscous forces
Stoney equation for thin film stress
Paris law for fatigue crack growth
Darcy friction factor for pipe flow

multi_constraint_queries.query: simulations with pressure above 200 kPa and velocity above 10 m/s
experiments with sample mass below 1 g and test duration under 10 minutes
tests with stress above 300 MPa and specimen thickness under 5 mm
CFD cases with pressure over 100 kPa and mesh spacing below 2 mm
atmospheric observations with pressure near 100 kPa and wind speed above 5 m/s
material tests with mass above 10 g and duration above 1 hour
pipe or channel flow with diameter 20-100 mm and velocity 0.5-5 m/s
simulations with time step below 0.01 s and pressure above 50 kPa
experiments with specimen length 100-500 mm and mass 1-100 g
